title: Exemplar entry: anthracycline cardiotoxicity + cardiac surveillance (internal, watermarked)
context: The end-to-end vertical-slice exemplar for ewing-survivorship-late-effects: one late-effect entry drafted through the full pipeline to prove the safety/provenance/licensing subsystem works before scaling content. It is HIGH risk-tier patient-facing material, so every assertion is sourced, the no-advice label is present, and the fact-vs-expression copyright firewall (synthesize facts and cite; never reproduce protected guideline text/tables/figures) holds. Because dual reviewers are not yet secured, it is held internal and watermarked, NOT published; no patient-facing page ships without BOTH a credentialed pediatric/AYA oncologist AND a patient-advocate version-scoped sign-off (neither alone suffices).
objective: Draft the anthracycline-cardiotoxicity + cardiac-surveillance entry end-to-end, fully sourced and passing all automated gates and the faithfulness eval, held as an internal watermarked artifact.
acceptanceCriteria: One late-effect entry drafted end-to-end, fully sourced (every assertion has a sourceRef), passing all automated gates and the faithfulness eval; exposure-keyed ('applies if you received an anthracycline')
Carries the 'Informational, not medical advice - consult your care team' label and correct red-flag/seek-care framing
Held internal + watermarked, NOT published (dual reviewers not yet secured); usable for reviewer recruitment
Kill-gate: if a faithful, fully-sourced, gate-passing exemplar cannot be produced without reproducing protected expression, stop and fix the pipeline before M2
